explanations.1: SHELL:=bash tells Make to use bash as the shell interpreter instead of the default /bin/sh, which means bash-specific features will work in your recipes.
explanations.4: default: help makes 'help' the target that runs when you type 'make' with no arguments, instead of the first target in the file.
explanations.6: COLOR_YELLOW:=33 stores the ANSI color code for yellow text; later you'll use this number inside escape sequences to colorize terminal output.
explanations.8: .PHONY: bash tells Make that 'bash' is not a real file to create, just a label for a command you want to run.
explanations.10: This printf command uses \e[1;${COLOR_YELLOW}m to start colored text and \e[0m to reset it back to normal, so the message prints in bold yellow.
